description_long: This object consists of three identical knives, each featuring a long, straight-edged blade with a slight upward curve near the tip and a subtle serration on the spine. The blades are metallic and have a matte, slightly reflective finish. Each knife has a contoured handle designed for ergonomic grip, with finger grooves and a visible color difference: one handle is brown, one is black, and one is olive green. The handles appear to be made of a textured polymer or plastic, providing a secure, non-slip surface. The knives lack any visible guard or crossguard, and there are no decorative elements or engravings on either the blades or handles. These knives are typical of utility or survival knives, suitable for use in kitchens, garages, or outdoor urban environments.
description: A set of three utility knives with straight, slightly curved metallic blades and contoured, colored handles. The handles are brown, black, and olive green, each designed for a secure grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the three knives, highlighting the blade shape and the distinct handle colors.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the slim profile of the blades and the width of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the blade length and handle contours, with handle colors visible.
description_view_3: Edge-on view focusing on the thickness of the blades and the ergonomic curvature of the handles.
attribute_blade_type: straight, single-edged, slight upward curve, partial spine serration
attribute_handle_design: contoured polymer with finger grooves, matte finish, colored (brown, black, olive green)
